marketState: neutral-bearish; price slightly above fair value with demand spike offsetting weak seasonality and storage surplus
summary: Front-month NG at $3.288 sits $0.33 above fair value ($2.958) and in the upper half of the fair-value band ($2.46–$3.46), with only $0.17 of room to the band ceiling — a mildly asymmetric setup favouring mean reversion. Seasonality is clearly bearish: only 19% probability of being higher in four weeks, a typical -1.8% four-week decline, and LOW historical reliability this week (51pp 10–90 spread). Current realised weather demand is exceptionally elevated at 152.8 Bcf/d (+30% vs 5y normal, near the top of the 7-day range), but granular 1–16 day demand forecasts are unavailable, limiting confidence in whether this heat-driven demand spike sustains. Storage remains +153 Bcf above the 5y average — a persistent fundamental overhang — while managed-money positioning at the 17th 5y COT percentile (net short -84,909 contracts) represents an extreme speculative short that creates meaningful squeeze risk and offsets some of the structural bearish case.

longTerm.invalidation: Weekly close above $3.48 (through fair-value band ceiling); storage surplus widens to +200 Bcf or more vs 5y average fails to develop (i.e. demand-driven drawdowns); COT managed-money net flips toward the 30th+ percentile as shorts cover
